event: TAVR with 26 mm CoreValve™ performed under general anesthesia
temporary transvenous pacing (TVP) wires placed in right ventricle (RV)
TVP wires placed through left common femoral vein
temporary pacemaker set as VVI, rate 50 beats/min, threshold 10 mA
two-dimensional transthoracic echocardiography (TTE) on postoperative day (POD) 1
moderate pericardial effusion around RV/right atrium
excessive respiratory variation of mitral inflow velocities
presumed diagnosis of RV rupture by temporary pacing wires
repeat TTE showed no new pericardial effusion
patient developed acute symptoms of diaphoresis
emergent TTE revealed reaccumulation of pericardial fluid
aortic root aortogram showed no leakage or tear
transesophageal echocardiography confirmed good valve function
drain left in place for 72 hours
computed tomography of chest negative for fluid reaccumulation
vital signs monitored continuously for 4 hours
transferred from ICU to step-down after 24 hours
